channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Prosecutor says Rihanna, family were home when woman charged with attempted murder fired
item.description: Rihanna, her partner A$AP Rocky, their three children and her mother were all at home when a woman now charged with attempted murder is alleged to have fired at the property, a prosecutor said.
item.content:encoded:
Rihanna, her partner A$AP Rocky, their three children and her mother were all at home when a woman now charged with attempted murder is alleged to have fired at the property, a prosecutor said.

(Image credit: Damian Dovarganes)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Why it took 60 years for a Vietnam vet to get the Medal of Honor
item.description: Paris Davis' was among the first Black men to lead green berets. His service in Vietnam would earn him the medal of honor, only it took nearly 60 years for him to receive the award.
item.content:encoded: Paris Davis' was among the first Black men to lead green berets. His service in Vietnam would earn him the medal of honor, only it took nearly 60 years for him to receive the award.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Seeing the need, more cities and states are stepping up to help provide child care
item.description: New York City isn't the only place pushing to expand free child care. We check in on this growing trend, and how states and cities are paying for it.
item.content:encoded: New York City isn't the only place pushing to expand free child care. We check in on this growing trend, and how states and cities are paying for it.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Scotland, Conn.: The town with six zip codes
item.description: Scotland, Conn., can be a confusing place to live. The tiny town has six zip codes, which makes receiving mail an unwelcome adventure.
item.content:encoded: Scotland, Conn., can be a confusing place to live. The tiny town has six zip codes, which makes receiving mail an unwelcome adventure.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Brone

item.title: Wheelchair curler Steve Emt's path from drunk driver to three-time Paralympian
item.description: Steve Emt and Laura Dwyer represent the U.S. in the Paralympics' new mixed doubles wheelchair curling event. They could bring home Team USA's first wheelchair curling medal ever.
item.content:encoded:
Steve Emt and Laura Dwyer represent the U.S. in the Paralympics' new mixed doubles wheelchair curling event. They could bring home Team USA's first wheelchair curling medal ever.

(Image credit: Maja Hitij)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: He was detained for 104 days. A year later, he's still fighting for the right to free speech
item.description: The case of Khalil, who was detained last March, sits at the vanguard of a battle of immigrants' due process and civil rights, and the Trump administration's mass detention and deportation policies.
item.content:encoded:
The case of Khalil, who was detained last March, sits at the vanguard of a battle of immigrants' due process and civil rights, and the Trump administration's mass detention and deportation policies.

(Image credit: Stephanie Keith)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Out of work and with 2 teens, this mom may lose food stamps under Trump's changes
item.description: Policy experts say new SNAP changes don't address the challenges faced by single parents. They also argue that losing food assistance will only create more barriers for struggling families.
item.content:encoded:
Policy experts say new SNAP changes don't address the challenges faced by single parents. They also argue that losing food assistance will only create more barriers for struggling families.

(Image credit: Caroline Yang for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: With Noem out, North Carolina hopes to get FEMA funding
item.description: Politicians in North Carolina are hopeful new leadership at the Department of Homeland Security will result in finally getting FEMA recovery funding that's been delayed by Kristi Noem.
item.content:encoded: Politicians in North Carolina are hopeful new leadership at the Department of Homeland Security will result in finally getting FEMA recovery funding that's been delayed by Kristi Noem.
item.dc:creator: Gerard Albert III

item.title: Scotland, Connecticut: The town with six ZIP codes
item.description: Scotland, Conn., can be a confusing place to live. The tiny town has six ZIP codes, which makes receiving mail an unwelcome adventure.
item.content:encoded: Scotland, Conn., can be a confusing place to live. The tiny town has six ZIP codes, which makes receiving mail an unwelcome adventure.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Brone

item.title: ISIS-inspired suspects charged after foiled New York protest attack
item.description: Two suspects are charged with attempting to provide material support to ISIS in connection with explosive device attacks near the official resident of New York City's mayor.
item.content:encoded: Two suspects are charged with attempting to provide material support to ISIS in connection with explosive device attacks near the official resident of New York City's mayor.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann
